factors.name: Treasury Yield Surge & Real Rate Re-pricing
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yield posted largest weekly rise since April 2025 (+23.5 bps to ~4.599%), driven by inflation shock fears. Higher nominal and real yields compress long-duration growth multiples that NAS100 heavily relies on.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations Spike
factors.summary: Top economic forecasters project inflation to hit 6% in Q2 2026 (per Friday survey); gold crashed >2.3% and silver collapsed 7.9% on inflation/rate hike fears. Fed funds futures now price rate hike probability as soon as December, signaling hawkish re-pricing.

factors.name: Fed Chair Transition & Powell Exit Amid Bond Rout
factors.summary: Jerome Powell named chair pro tempore as Kevin Warsh incoming; transition occurs amid one of the 'wildest Fed eras in history' with bond market under pressure. Leadership uncertainty + hawkish bias from Warsh camp compounds growth-stock headwinds.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran/Strait of Hormuz)
factors.summary: Trump-China talks failed to 'pry open Strait of Hormuz'; Persian Gulf energy tensions persist with Adnoc LNG tankers masking location. Oil-driven inflation shock and flight-to-safety bid pressures risk appetite, particularly growth equities.

factors.name: USD Strength & DXY Rally
factors.summary: US Dollar Index climbs to 5-week high (above 99.30) on hawkish Fed expectations; stronger dollar headwind for NAS100 given heavy international revenue exposure from mega-cap tech exports.

factors.name: Arm Holdings Antitrust Probe
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports Arm Holdings faces US antitrust investigation over chip technology; adds regulatory overhang to semiconductor ecosystem critical to NDX performance.

factors.name: Berkshire Hathaway Trimming Mega-Cap Tech
factors.summary: Berkshire sold ~$8B Chevron (Q1), exited Amazon, UnitedHealth, Visa, Mastercard entirely; while bought Delta $2.6B, the net reduction in mega-cap tech/financial exposure signals value rotation out of growth.

factors.name: IPO Speculation (SpaceX, Cerebras, Forbright)
factors.summary: Cerebras IPO blockbuster debut but then fell 10% day 2; SpaceX targeting June 11 IPO on Nasdaq. Jim Cramer warns SpaceX IPO could be 'destructive' for broader market due to speculative demand. Mixed signal on liquidity/sentiment.

factors.name: Market Breadth & Index Performance
factors.summary: Friday close: Nasdaq fell -410.08 pts (-1.54%), Russell 2000 led declines. Weekly gains erased. NAS100 -0.67% in 4h, -0.22% in 24h indicate weakness in progress but not yet extended.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings & Guidance Pipeline
factors.summary: No major earnings releases in immediate news window; next cycle will be critical. Hyperscaler capex outlook (AWS, Azure, GCP) remains undefined pending earnings calls. Cannot assess near-term catalysts from this screen.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in early-stage downward price action amid a sharp repricing of rate expectations. The catalyst is a perfect storm: inflation forecasts rising to 6% in Q2, Treasury yields surging +23.5 bps on the week to ~4.599%, and Fed leadership transition amid a hawkish bias (Warsh incoming, Powell exiting). Real yields—the biggest anchor for long-duration growth—are rising sharply, compressing valuations across mega-cap tech. Geopolitical risk (Iran/Strait) and USD strength add headwinds. Berkshire's recent trimming of mega-cap tech positions and hints of value rotation are secondary but notable. The move is in Phase 2 (underway but not yet extended), with -0.67% in 4h and -1.54% Nasdaq on Friday suggesting sellers have control but room to extend.
catalysts_ahead: Fed speakers & Warsh confirmation process (regulatory/leadership clarity)
CPI/Core PCE releases (inflation data critical to rate path; could flip bias if cooler)
FOMC decision timing (next meeting risk)
Major mega-cap earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA) — capex guidance essential
Geopolitical escalation or de-escalation (Iran conflict)
Treasury auction or yield curve flattening (if real yields stabilize, growth relief)
screening.risk_warning: If CPI surprise comes in COOLER than expected, or Powell/Warsh signal dovish pause on rate hikes, the entire bearish thesis unwinds quickly and NAS100 rebounds 1-2%. Real yield momentum is the most fragile link; any hint of Fed patience or recession risk flips sentiment bullish for growth. SPX breaking support below 7,400 could also trigger capitulation that reverses the tone.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is SELL, but move is in Phase 2 (underway, not extended). Wait for retest of 29,300–29,400 resistance before shorting; consider tactical entry on intraday bounce into resistance if confirmed by higher close + breakdown of 29,000 support on follow-through. Avoid chasing lower into close; major event risk (CPI, FOMC timing) within 24–72h caps aggressive positioning.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Move underway with -1.54% close; wait for failed bounce toward 29400-29500 resistance or fresh breakdown below 29000 for safer entries.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Surging
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yield posted largest weekly rise since April 2025, climbing 23.5 bps (5.39%) in one week to 4.599% high per investinglive. Long-duration tech multiples compress significantly under rising nominal rates.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations Shift Hawkish
factors.summary: Fed funds futures now pricing in rate HIKE as soon as December per CNBC; traders fear second wave of inflation from prolonged US-Iran conflict. Jerome Powell exits as chair pro tempore, Kevin Warsh incoming; hawkish repricing underway.

factors.name: Inflation Fears Resurging
factors.summary: Top forecasters project inflation rate hitting 6% in Q2 per CNBC; oil market tensions from Strait of Hormuz and Iran conflict fueling fresh inflation shock. Growth stocks highly sensitive to inflation repricing.

factors.name: Bond Market Rout
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'Global Bond Rout Catches Up With Wall Street's Risk Rally'; bond market stress typically precedes equity multiple compression, especially in high-valuation tech.

factors.name: Price Action: Tech Leading Declines
factors.summary: NASDAQ closed -410.08 pts (-1.54%) at 26,225.14, worst performer among major indices; Russell 2000 also weak. Declines erased entire week's gains per investinglive.

factors.name: Arm Holdings Antitrust Probe
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports Arm Holdings to face US antitrust probe over chip tech; regulatory overhang on semiconductor sector, a key NDX component group.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY climbs to five-week high above 99.30 per FXStreet on hawkish Fed bets; stronger dollar headwind for NDX components with heavy international revenue exposure (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL).

factors.name: Berkshire Tech Positioning
factors.summary: Berkshire bought more Alphabet but shed Amazon, Visa, Mastercard per filings; mixed signal on tech conviction from major institutional player. George Soros's fund bought Berkshire, boosted NVDA and AAPL stakes.

factors.name: China-US Relations
factors.summary: Trump says China to buy 200 Boeing jets after Xi visit; some constructive tone but Trump also said China-Taiwan should 'both cool it'. Mixed implications for tech supply chains and US-China tech trade.

factors.name: Institutional Semi Flows
factors.summary: Institutional investors flocked to establish new stakes in semiconductor firms in Q1 per Stock Market News; underlying demand thesis remains intact despite near-term rate headwinds.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Speculation
factors.summary: SpaceX targets June 11 IPO pricing on Nasdaq per CoinDesk; Cramer fears 'destructive' for rest of market. Could pull liquidity from existing tech names if retail flows chase new shiny object.

factors.name: AI Chip Competition
factors.summary: Cerebras stock fell 10% in first full day after blockbuster IPO debut; signals both strong AI demand but also increased competition to NVDA's dominance.

factors.name: Options Positioning / Vol
factors.summary: No explicit VIX data in feed but bond rout, rate repricing, and -1.54% NDX close suggest elevated volatility and risk-off positioning likely building.

confirmation.narrative: Nasdaq 100 closed down 1.54% as a resurgent bond market rout and hawkish Fed repricing crushed tech multiples. The 10-year yield surged 23.5 bps this week to 4.599%, its largest weekly jump since April 2025, driven by fears of a second inflation wave from prolonged US-Iran conflict and oil supply disruption. Fed funds futures now price a rate HIKE by December, a dramatic shift from the easing bias priced weeks ago. Long-duration growth stocks are acutely vulnerable to this dual shock of rising nominal rates and inflation repricing, erasing the week's gains in a single session.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh confirmation and first policy signals (timing unclear)
Japan Q1 GDP release (weekend/Monday Asia) - energy shock inflation implications
Singapore NODX, Malaysia data - Asia tech export demand indicators
Any escalation or de-escalation in US-Iran Strait of Hormuz tensions
Next US CPI/PCE print (not in 24h window but critical for validating 6% Q2 inflation forecast)
SpaceX IPO filing June 11 (potential liquidity drain from existing tech)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if oil prices collapse on rapid Iran conflict resolution, taking inflation expectations down with it and allowing Fed to return to easing bias. Also invalidated if 10-year yield reverses sharply below 4.35% on flight-to-quality or if mega-cap earnings (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL) in coming weeks show AI capex acceleration that overrides rate concerns. Technically, sustained reclaim above 29500 would signal buyers stepping in.

reason: DISAGREEMENT: Haiku=SELL vs Sonnet=BEARISH — silent log

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield Surge
factors.summary: 10-year yield posted largest weekly rise since April 2025, climbing 23.5 bps to 4.599%. Higher nominal yields compress long-duration growth multiples in Nasdaq-heavy index.

factors.name: Inflation Shock / Re-Rating Risk
factors.summary: Top economic forecasters projecting CPI to hit 6% in Q2 2026. Fed funds futures now pricing rate hike as soon as December 2026, marking shift from dovish expectations. Persistent inflation fears driving bond selloff.

factors.name: Real Yields (TIPS)
factors.summary: Real yields rising in tandem with nominal yields amid inflation concerns. Tech / high-duration growth assets extremely sensitive to positive real rate moves—headwind for NDX.

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: Dollar Index (DXY) climbed to five-week high (99.30+) on Friday; now at highest level since April 8. Stronger USD pressures international revenue for mega-cap tech (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL) and weakens emerging-market tech demand.

factors.name: Risk-Off Positioning / Selloff Momentum
factors.summary: Major US indices closed lower Friday; Nasdaq fell -410 bps (-1.54%). Weekly declines erased prior week's gains. Equities and bonds both sold off, signaling broad risk-off shift.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tension (Iran / Middle East)
factors.summary: Multiple sources cite US-Iran brinkmanship hitting wall; ADNOC loading LNG onto dark tankers in Persian Gulf. Prolonged hostilities could trigger second inflation wave, forcing further rate hikes. Gold and commodities volatile on these fears.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings / Guidance Pipeline
factors.summary: No major Q1 earnings surprises or forward guidance in immediate window. Trump disclosed tech stock purchases (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, BROADCOM) but no new catalysts from company results.

factors.name: Fed Leadership Transition
factors.summary: Powell exits as chair pro tempore; Kevin Warsh to assume chairmanship. Transition timing amid volatile bond market and inflation surge introduces policy uncertainty but no immediate hawkish/dovish signal.

factors.name: AI / Hyperscaler Capex Cycle
factors.summary: SpaceX targeting June 11 IPO filing (Nasdaq listing); semiconductor demand remains strong (Taiwan tech exports sustaining growth). Cerebras IPO blockbuster despite Q1 selloff suggests underlying AI-chip appetite intact.

factors.name: Antitrust Headwinds
factors.summary: ARM Holdings facing US antitrust probe over chip technology. Regulatory scrutiny on mega-cap tech remains elevated risk factor for NDX components.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a risk-off unwind driven by a sharp inflation re-rating: 10Y yields surged to 4.599% this week (largest weekly move since April 2025), real yields climbed, and the USD Index hit five-week highs. Fed futures now price a rate hike as soon as December 2026 after forecasters project CPI to reach 6% in Q2—a stark reversal from dovish expectations. Friday's broad selloff (Nasdaq -1.54%) erased the week's prior gains, signaling momentum has shifted to the downside. While mega-cap tech fundamentals (AI capex cycle, strong export demand) remain solid, the combination of inflation shock + higher real yields + stronger dollar is toxic for long-duration growth equities. This is early-stage Phase 2: the move down has started but is not yet extended.
catalysts_ahead: PCE inflation print (next major datapoint expected mid-week)
Fed speakers (new chair Warsh confirmation/commentary)
Q1 earnings from AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA (dates TBD)
SpaceX IPO filing / roadshow (targeting June)
Treasury auctions and bond market stability tests
China-Taiwan tensions escalation risk
geopolitical spillover from Iran-US tensions
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidation: If CPI surprise comes in COOL (below 3.5%) and/or Fed signals pivot toward cuts, real yields could collapse, triggering sharp reversal higher in NDX. Also: strong earnings beat + raised guidance from mega-cap tech could override macro headwinds and re-ignite risk-on. Supply/demand imbalances in oil/energy could escalate geopolitical premium and force new inflation shock upward. Monitor 10Y yield—if it rolls over below 4.45%, that could signal short-term bounce for NDX.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (momentum downward but not extended). Wait for retest of resistance near 29,400 or a bounce to prior day's highs before considering fresh shorts. For longs: pullback to support around 28,800–28,900 offers lower-risk entry if inflation narrative softens or real yields stabilize.

factors.name: Real Yields Surge
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yield posted largest weekly rise since April 2025, climbing 23.5 bps to 4.599%. Higher real yields compress long-duration tech multiples; Nasdaq mega-caps extremely sensitive.

factors.name: Inflation Surprise & Fed Repricing
factors.summary: Top forecasters project CPI hitting 6% in Q2 2026 per MarketWatch. Fed futures now pricing rate hikes as soon as December, reversing prior cut expectations. Powell exits as chair amid bond market surge.

factors.name: Geopolitical Oil/Energy Shock
factors.summary: Iran strait tensions, Trump-Xi talks yield no resolution on Iran conflict. ADNOC loading LNG with tankers masking position; oil supply risk fueling inflation fears and equity selloff.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY climbed to 5-week high (99.30+) on Friday, reaching highest since April 8. Stronger dollar headwind for NDX components with heavy international revenue; also pressures risk assets.

factors.name: Equity Selloff Underway
factors.summary: Friday close: Nasdaq -1.54% (-410 pts to 26,225), S&P 500 -1.24%, erasing week's gains. Selloff accelerated into close on inflation/yield fears; NAS100 down 0.67% intraday 4h, -0.22% 24h shows early momentum shift.

factors.name: AI/Semiconductor Demand Still Bid
factors.summary: Cerebras IPO blockbuster debut Thursday signals unstoppable AI chip demand. Samsung AI boom spurs labor action (not negative for sentiment). Institutional investors increased semiconductor holdings in Q1 per filings.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Filing Imminent
factors.summary: SpaceX targets June 11 IPO pricing on Nasdaq; 5-for-1 stock split approved. Potential catalyst for speculative inflows, but Jim Cramer warns IPO market 'destructive' for broader market sentiment.

factors.name: Fed Leadership Transition Risk
factors.summary: Powell named interim chair pro tempore until Warsh sworn in. Leadership vacuum amid 'one of wildest Fed eras in history' creates policy uncertainty; market structure fragile into transition.

factors.name: Antitrust Headwind on Mega-Caps
factors.summary: ARM Holdings faces US antitrust probe over chip tech per Bloomberg. Potential for broader scrutiny on NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL supply chain dominance.

factors.name: China-Taiwan Tensions Contained
factors.summary: Trump says China and Taiwan should 'both cool it'; no new escalation reported. De-escalation narrative helps, but uncertainty remains on US arms sales to Taiwan.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is in early Phase 2 selloff driven by the confluence of real yield expansion (10Y at 4.60%, +23.5 bps weekly), re-priced Fed rate hike expectations (now pricing hikes by December), and geopolitical oil/energy shock (Iran strait tensions). Friday's 1.54% selloff erased the week's gains and compressed long-duration tech multiples sharply. Although AI capex and semiconductor demand remain fundamentally strong (Cerebras IPO success, institutional accumulation), the immediate macro backdrop—inflation surprise, hawkish Fed repricing, and dollar strength—is overwhelmingly bearish for growth equities. The move is underway but not extended (only -0.67% 4h, -0.22% 24h); Phase 2 suggests further downside is likely on Monday/early week unless yields stabilize below 4.55%.
catalysts_ahead: Monday opens: Yield market repricing, potential gap down if 10Y stays >4.55%
This week: PMI data, central bank events per FXStreet calendar
Q1 earnings revisions/guidance for mega-caps (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL)
Fed Warsh confirmation hearing (policy direction clarity)
CPI release timing (if scheduled early-mid week, major re-pricing event)
screening.risk_warning: If Treasury yields roll over sharply below 4.50% on safe-haven demand or Fed pivot signals, the bearish case collapses and Nasdaq rebounds hard. Also, strong AI earnings surprise from NVDA or MSFT could override macro headwinds. Geopolitical de-escalation (Iran) or unexpected Fed dovish pivot would be immediate flip to BUY.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2; avoid chasing shorts into a 1%+ gap. Wait for retest of 29,300–29,500 resistance on Monday bounce, or for yields to hold above 4.55% to confirm breakdown. Short on rejection of resistance, or scale in on further 1–1.5% decline to 28,600–28,800 support.
